site.name: Asher
site.title: Asher — small, honest things for the web
site.description: Personal site of Asher. Designer & engineer based in Shanghai. Projects, writing, and notes on design and engineering.

home.introTail: , based in Shanghai. I keep this corner of the internet as a journal — projects, half-thoughts, and the occasional rant.
home.nowStatus: now — shipping personal-site v2 & rewriting the about page (again)

bento.portfolioDesc: Twelve projects, mostly web & mobile. Some are products, some are essays that happen to ship as code.
bento.blogDesc: On building this site, why I dropped Contentlayer, one opinion about icon buttons.
bento.aboutDesc: Ten years of designing and shipping things. Currently independent, occasionally open to work.
bento.resourcesDesc: Linear, Cursor, Figma — and a small list of books I actually re-read.

about.greeting: about me ✿ in a few words
about.lede: Hello. I'm Asher — a designer and engineer based in Shanghai. I've spent the last decade quietly making things for the web; this is where I keep notes about
about.longerBody1: I came to this work through writing first. Long before I knew what
about.longerBody3: , I was obsessing over which paragraph break felt right. The instincts stayed; the medium just shifted.
about.longerBody4: These days I split my time between client engagements (mostly design systems and small product surfaces) and self-initiated work that lives here. I prefer projects where I can do both the design and the engineering — the seam between the two is where the interesting decisions hide.

timeline.note: Design systems, product surfaces, the occasional brand audit. Recent partners: two startups and a public cultural institution.
Led the design system rebuild. Shipped the consumer iOS app from v1 to v3.
Owned dashboards and the data-export pipeline. Wrote a lot of CSS.

timeline.where: freelance, a design school, one bad agency
timeline.note: The years I learned what I never wanted to do again.

values.title: Honest placeholders.
values.body: A grey block with a label is *more useful* than a fake chart. Pretend less.

values.title: One detail at 120%.
values.body: *Everything else at 80%.* Taste is knowing where to spend the effort.

values.title: Static beats dynamic.
values.body: Until proven otherwise. *Most pages* don't need to be interactive.

values.title: Done is a feature.
values.body: *Ship the small thing.* Then ship the next one.

about.ctaSentence: Currently open to new projects. Drop a note via

portfolio.metaDescription: A small catalogue of things I've made.
portfolio.ledeTail: . Some are products, some are design systems, some are essays that happen to ship as code.

blog.metaDescription: Journal & rants — on design, engineering, and the in-between.
blog.ledeTail: . Mostly short, occasionally long, always rewritten three times before they land here.

project.onTheBenchBody: Case studies are short on purpose. Want the long version? Email me — I'll tell you what didn't work, too.

resources.metaDescription: Things I read, use, and quietly recommend.
resources.lede: A working list of the tools, books, and people that have shaped how I think.
resources.sectionToolsBlurb: things I'd genuinely have a worse day without
resources.sectionBooksBlurb: books I return to, not ones I finished first

tools.name: Linear
tools.reason: Respects keyboard users. Pricing got worse, value still high.

tools.name: Cursor
tools.reason: Where most of my code now happens. The AI is genuinely useful; the UI doesn't get in the way.

tools.name: Figma
tools.reason: Still no real competitor. Even dev mode has earned its place.

tools.name: Velite
tools.reason: Type-safe MDX without the Contentlayer drama. Powers this site.

books.name: White
books.reason: The clearest argument for restraint I've ever read. Read it slowly; it's 100 pages and you'll need them.

books.name: Grid Systems
books.reason: The bible. Skip the rhetoric, study the diagrams.

books.name: Refactoring UI
books.reason: Practical, mid-career, no-nonsense. Recommended for engineers who design.

books.name: A Pattern Language
books.reason: About buildings, but about everything. Architects, designers, town planners — pay attention.

essays.name: Worse Is Better
essays.reason: The original case for shipping. Still painful, still right.

essays.name: Why Generative Art Is Hard
essays.reason: A working artist's honest accounting of why this looks easy and isn't.

essays.name: Rauno Freiberg
essays.reason: For the experimental hero, the precise interaction, the designer-to-designer wink.

essays.name: Kenya Hara
essays.reason: The case study for restraint as a design philosophy, not a lack of effort.

contact.ledeTail: to reach me is email — the rest are slower channels I check less reverently.
contact.emailBody: The main channel. Use a clear subject line and I'll usually reply within a working day.
contact.twitterBody: For the half-formed thought and the occasional design rant.
contact.githubBody: Where most of the code lives.
contact.githubTail: are the love language of open source.
contact.linkedinBody: For the kind of message that probably should be an email. I check it on
contact.promise2: It might take a day, sometimes a week. But the inbox is never the void, and the door is never closed.
